sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, I can see that clicking on the left frame (at rows 33-35, cols 12-14) has changed its center from black to white (selected), and the right frame's center has changed from white to black (deselected). So the selection has switched from the right frame to the left frame.
Now the left frame is selected (white center) and the right frame is not selected (black center). Let me try to move the left frame. Since it's in the left target, maybe I need to move it out first, then do something else.
But wait - looking at the image more carefully, I notice something. The left frame (now with white center) is in the left panel, and the right frame (now with black center) is in the corridor. The left frame is at rows 33-35, cols 12-14, which is inside the left target at rows 32-36, cols 11-15.
Let me try moving the left frame. Since it's selected, arrow keys should move it. Let me try RIGHT to move it towards the barrier, or UP/DOWN to move it within the left panel.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the problem. The left frame is already in the left target. The right frame needs to go to the right target. But the barrier blocks the path.
Hmm, let me look at the image again. The left frame is now selected (white center). Let me try moving it RIGHT to see if it can move towards the barrier. If it can move through the barrier, then maybe the barrier only blocks the right frame, not the left frame.
Actually, that doesn't make sense. Both frames are the same type (green 3x3 frames).
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is not to move both frames to their respective targets. Maybe the goal is to move one frame to one target, and the other frame to the other target, but in a specific order or with specific conditions.
Or maybe the goal is to have both frames in the same target? Or maybe the goal is to have the frames in specific positions, not necessarily in the targets.
Let me try moving the left frame RIGHT to see what happens. If it can move through the barrier, that would be interesting.
Actually, let me first check if the left frame can move at all. Let me try RIGHT.
